title: Factorial (recursive)
prompt: Write factorial_rec(n) returning n! using recursion (the function calls itself). Base case: factorial_rec(0) is 1.

help.plain: A recursive function answers a big case by asking itself the same question about a smaller case, until the case is so small the answer is obvious.
help.analogy: Like a line of people each asking the person behind them their number, adding one, and passing it back — until the last person, who just knows their own. The answer bubbles back up the line.
help.mnemonic: BRS — Base case Returns, else Recurse Smaller. If the input is at the base, return the known answer; otherwise combine this step with the function called on a smaller input.
help.nudge: What is the one input where you know the answer without any more work? (For factorial, what is factorial_rec(0)?)

example.problem: Sum the numbers 1..n using recursion instead of a loop (a different task).
example.note: Same base-case-plus-smaller-call shape. Yours MULTIPLIES (n times the factorial of n-1) and bases out at 1, instead of adding and basing out at 0.
